description: Creates a 2D directed arrow between two points. Accepts point() expressions, point variables, or point tuples for endpoints. Supports position vectors via type(position) and polar form via type(polar). Can also use vector literals {x,y} for position with tuple arithmetic.
utility: Create a 2D vector (arrow) between two points, tuples, or using position/polar form

arguments.name: point1
arguments.description: Start point expression (point() or point variable)

arguments.name: point2
arguments.description: End point expression (point() or point variable)

alternates.description: Vector from point1 to point2. Each must be a point() expression or a point variable.

alternates.variant: Position vector from origin to a point
alternates.description: Position vector from origin (0,0) to the given point

alternates.variant: Position vector from origin to coordinates
alternates.description: Position vector from origin (0,0) to (x, y)
Vector from origin with given length and angle in degrees
Vector starting at given point with given length and angle in degrees

alternates.syntax: vector(G, length, angle, type(polar), ox, oy)
alternates.description: Vector starting at (ox, oy) with given length and angle in degrees

examples.scenario: Force vector F1 applied to a box in physics problem
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(2,29), 23.9, 29.1, range(-10, 10), range(-5, 5), type(plotting), grid(noaxes, nolines)); vector_1 = vector(graph_1, point(graph_1, -8, 1.1), point(graph_1, -3.6, 1.1))

examples.scenario: Second force vector F2 with label
examples.expression: vector_2 = vector(graph_1, point(graph_1, -7.9, -0.7), point(graph_1, -3.6, -0.7)); label_2 = label(graph_1, at(vector_2), "F_2 = 2\hat{i} + \hat{j} - \hat{k}", buff(-1.5, -0.5), fs(18), c(black))

examples.scenario: Resultant force vector F = F1 + F2
examples.expression: vector_3 = vector(graph_1, point(graph_1, -0.2, 0.6), point(graph_1, 4.3, 0.6)); label_3 = label(graph_1, at(vector_3), "F = F_1 + F_2", buff(-1.5, 1), fs(18), c(black))

examples.scenario: Three vectors forming a triangle between points
examples.expression:
graph_1=g2d(at(5,21),25,25,range(-1,5,1),range(-1,5,1),type(uniform))
point_1 = point(graph_1, 1, 1)
point_2 = point(graph_1, 4, 1)
point_3 = point(graph_1, 2, 2.3)
vector_1 = vector(graph_1, point_1, point_3, mid)
vector_2 = vector(graph_1, point_3, point_2, mid)
vector_3 = vector(graph_1, point_2, point_1, mid)

examples.scenario: Polar vector with length and angle
Vector from tuple arithmetic using vector literals
Vector using point tuples and vector function
